name: Pocket (Mozilla) Read-Later and Content API
what_it_does: Read-later service with REST API — enabling users to save web articles, videos, and pages for later reading with text extraction, tags, highlights, and content recommendations, with Mozilla backing providing a developer-friendly API for reading list management and content discovery integrations, built into Firefox browser.
use_cases: Saving URLs to Pocket reading list via API for browser extension, automation, and integration
Retrieving user's Pocket reading list with tags and metadata via API for PKM system integration
Organizing Pocket items with tags and archives via API for content curation workflow automation
Pulling Pocket highlights and annotations via API for note-taking system integration
Building content saving functionality into applications using Pocket API as backend
not_for: Team content sharing and collaboration (Pocket is single-user focused)
Enterprise document management (Pocket is for web article read-later, not documents)
AI-powered content analysis (use Readwise Reader for AI summaries)
best_when: A developer building reading list or content discovery integrations needs a large, established read-later user base to integrate with — especially for Firefox/Mozilla ecosystem integrations where Pocket is natively embedded.
avoid_when: You need team collaboration, AI summaries, PDF reading, or are building for enterprise knowledge management rather than personal reading lists.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code flow with Pocket-specific implementation (not standard OAuth2 — uses custom request token flow). Access token for user-delegated access. User-scoped access. No webhooks. REST API v3 for item management. Firefox native integration. No fine-grained scopes. Pocket API uses non-standard OAuth2 implementation requiring custom client implementation.

pricing.paid_tiers: Pocket Premium $4.99/month or $44.99/year (permanent library, full-text search, suggested tags, unlimited highlights)
pricing.notes: Originally Read It Later. Acquired by Mozilla in 2017. Integrated into Firefox as native feature. 150M+ registered users. Mozilla public benefit corporation ownership provides product stability. Growing competition from Readwise Reader, Matter, and Omnivore. Pocket content discovery (recommended articles) is editorial AI curation. Mozilla's commitment to web privacy aligns with Pocket's data handling. 2023: Firefox Pocket recommendations reduced in some markets. Open ecosystem philosophy but limited API investment.

performance.rate_limits: 1 request per second; contact for higher limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API v3 for reading list management. Custom OAuth2 implementation (non-standard flow). Good documentation on developer portal. No webhooks — polling required. Rate limit 1 req/sec. Mozilla-owned with GDPR compliance. Non-standard OAuth2 (custom request token flow) is gotcha for standard OAuth2 libraries.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Item reads fully idempotent. Archive and favorite operations idempotent. Item save is idempotent for same URL. Tag operations idempotent by item ID + tag.
known_agent_gotchas: Non-standard OAuth2 implementation — does not work with standard OAuth2 libraries without customization
Rate limit 1 req/sec — very restrictive for bulk operations (must use retrieve API with filters)
No webhooks — must poll for reading list changes
Mozilla public benefit corporation — product direction may not optimize for developer features
US data residency — EU customers on GDPR adequacy (Privacy Shield successor)

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant (Mozilla EU obligations). US data residency. Custom OAuth2 implementation. Mozilla public benefit corp with privacy-first values. Reading list data includes user browsing and reading habits — privacy-sensitive. Mozilla's privacy commitments are strongest in industry. Non-standard OAuth consumer key/secret must be secured. No scope granularity — full reading list access per OAuth grant.
